title: class2_candidate_set_schema
description: LLM-generated bounded candidate set for Class 2 clarification. Wraps the candidate list with provenance and bounded-variability metadata. This payload is internal evidence between LocalLlmAdapter and Class2ClarificationManager; it does not authorize execution. Each candidate is converted by the manager into a ClarificationChoice and ultimately surfaced via clarification_interaction_schema. Authority boundary (02_safety_and_authority_boundaries.md §4): the LLM may suggest, the deterministic validator decides admissibility, and the canonical low-risk catalog gates execution.

candidate_source.description: Provenance of the candidate set. llm_generated means produced by LocalLlmAdapter.generate_class2_candidates() within the bounded-variability constraints; default_fallback means the manager's static _DEFAULT_CANDIDATES table was used (e.g. on LLM failure or constraint violation).

candidates.description: Bounded candidate list. Each item maps directly to a ClarificationChoice. action_hint/target_hint are optional but, when present, must reference values that the deterministic validator can admit against low_risk_actions.json.

prompt.description: User-facing TTS prompt. Length cap is the conversational-prompt accessibility ceiling; longer prompts must be rejected so the static fallback is used.

action_hint.description: Suggested low-risk action. When present (CLASS_1 candidates), must be admissible by low_risk_actions.json or the candidate is rejected during validation.

target_hint.description: Suggested target device. When present (CLASS_1 candidates), must be in the allowed_targets list for the chosen action_hint.

generated_at_ms.description: Wall-clock generation timestamp in epoch milliseconds.

model_id.description: LLM model identifier (e.g. 'llama3.2', 'mock'). For default_fallback this should be the literal 'static_default_fallback'.

prompt_constraints_applied.description: Echo of the bounded-variability constraints applied during validation. Recorded for audit so a reviewer can see what the LLM was held to.

rejection_reason.description: When candidate_source=default_fallback, an optional short reason explaining why the LLM output was rejected (e.g. 'prompt_too_long', 'action_hint_not_in_catalog', 'invalid_json'). Useful for evaluation in Package A.
